scenes.text: Where does startup money come from? And what do investors actually buy?
Imagine your company is a pizza. The whole thing belongs to you, the founder.
How much is your pizza worth? That's your valuation. Maybe a million dollars. Maybe ten.
An investor says: I'll give you cash. In return, I want a slice.
Each funding round is a bigger pizza, and a higher price. Pre-seed. Seed. Series A.
Yes, your slice gets smaller. But the pizza gets way bigger. Smaller slice. Way more worth.
Smaller slice. Bigger pie. Bigger life.
